arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta insists Ethan Nwaneri will get more chances for Arsenal this season
arts.sum: The highly-rated midfielder delivered on his first start for the Gunners after he struck in both halves.

arts.ttl: 16-year-old becomes second youngest player in Arsenal history
arts.sum: Jack Porter has become the second youngest player in Arsenal history after being named in the starting lineup for Wednesday night's Carabao Cup clash with Bolton Wanderers. First choice goalkeeper David Raya suffered a thigh injury during the weekend's 2-2 draw with Manchester City.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta gives David Raya injury update after 16-year-old makes Arsenal history
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta admits that he does not know whether David Raya will be fit for this weekend's Premier League clash with Leicester City.

arts.ttl: Papers: Arsenal weigh up move for Leverkusen star Wirtz
arts.sum: The top stories and transfer rumours from Thursday's newspapers...

arts.ttl: Haaland controversy, red card call - Final Michael Oliver verdict given after Man City vs Arsenal - football.london
arts.sum: A Premier League panel has given their verdict on Michael Oliver's decision to issue a red card to Leandro Trossard during Arsenal's draw with Manchester City. A report in ESPN has provided details on a review conducted by the Premier League's Key Match Incidents Panel (KMI).

arts.ttl: Aaron Ramsdale shows true colours with three-word message to Arsenal wonderkid
arts.sum: Arsenal were forced to play 16-year-old Jack Porter in their Carabao Cup victory against Bolton Wanderers and former Gunners goalkeeper Aaron Ramsdale had some kind words for the youngster

arts.ttl: Carabao Cup fourth-round draw in full as Man Utd, Liverpool and Arsenal discover fate
arts.sum: The draw for the fourth round of the Carabao Cup has been completed, with Manchester United, Manchester City and Chelsea among the clubs to learn their fate for the October fixtures

arts.ttl: 'That's what football's all about': Sky Sports pundit in awe by £45m Arsenal star's 'brilliance' vs Bolton
Arsenal player ratings vs Bolton: Ethan Nwaneri enjoys dream night as Raheem Sterling display bodes well
arts.sum: Youthful Arsenal side delivered as rotated cast took chance

arts.ttl: Ethan Nwaneri and Jack Porter take the limelight in Arsenal’s win over Bolton
arts.sum: Nwaneri netted twice while Declan Rice, Raheem Sterling and Kai Havertz were also on target.
